Interface GraphicsManager

All Superinterfaces:
AutoCloseable, NonThrowingAutoCloseable
All Known Implementing Classes:
OpenGLGraphicsManager
public interface GraphicsManager extends NonThrowingAutoCloseable
Manages rendering contexts and their lifecycle.

Note: Do not manually close image instances returned by a graphics manager. Instead, use the destroyImage(Path) method for proper cleanup.

See Also:
  • RenderingContext

    Method
    Description
    void
    close()
    This method is not expected to throw any checked exceptions.

    Creates a new rendering context with a WindowSurface and a stage created using the given creator.

    Creates a new rendering context with a WindowSurface and a stage created using the given creator.

    If an image is loaded at the given path, closes and removes it, otherwise does nothing.

    Returns an existing rendering context bound to the given stage.

    Returns an image previously loaded from the given path, if any, otherwise returns null.

    Schedules the given task to run in the graphics loop.

    tick()
    Advances the graphics manager by one tick.

  • Method Details

    • createWindowContext

      default RenderingContext createWindowContext(String title, StageCreator stageCreator)
      Creates a new rendering context with a WindowSurface and a stage created using the given creator.

      The window will be initially hidden, non-resizable and non-fullscreen in the default implementation, and have the size of the Stage created using the given StageCreator. The window can be shown with RenderingSurface.show().

      Parameters:
      title - initial window title
      stageCreator - a stage creation strategy
      Returns:
      a new rendering context
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if graphics manager is closed
      NullPointerException - if any argument is null

    • createWindowContext

      RenderingContext createWindowContext(String title, StageCreator stageCreator, boolean translucent, boolean fullscreen)
      Creates a new rendering context with a WindowSurface and a stage created using the given creator.

      The window will be initially hidden and non-resizable in the default implementation, and have the size of the Stage created using the given StageCreator. The window can be shown with RenderingSurface.show().

      Parameters:
      title - window title
      stageCreator - a stage creation strategy
      translucent - whether the window should be transparent (not changeable after creation!)
      fullscreen - whether the window should be fullscreen
      Returns:
      a new rendering context
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if graphics manager is closed
      NullPointerException - if any argument is null

    • createEmbeddedContext

      RenderingContext createEmbeddedContext(EmbeddedSurface surface, StageCreator stageCreator)
      Creates a new rendering context with the given embedded rendering surface and a stage created using the given creator.

      To create a context with a window surface, use createWindowContext(...).

      Parameters:
      surface - the surface to render on
      stageCreator - a stage creation strategy
      Returns:
      a new rendering context
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if graphics manager is closed
      NullPointerException - if any argument is null

    • getContext

      RenderingContext getContext(Stage boundStage)
      Returns an existing rendering context bound to the given stage.
      Parameters:
      boundStage - the bound stage
      Returns:
      the corresponding rendering context
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if stage is null

    • hasActiveContexts

      boolean hasActiveContexts()
      Checks whether this manager currently has any active contexts.
      Returns:
      whether there are active contexts

    • loadImage

      Image loadImage(Path path, boolean autoLoad)
      Loads an image at the specified path. The image will automatically be closed with this manager. If an image is already loaded at the given path, it is closed, removed and replaced with a new one. The sprite/stage assets are loaded automatically, so there's usually no need to call this method manually.
      Parameters:
      path - the file path
      autoLoad - whether to preload the image to all existing & future rendering contexts
      Returns:
      the image
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if graphics manager is closed

    • hasLoadedImage

      boolean hasLoadedImage(Path path)
      Determines whether an image is already loaded at the given path. This can be useful to prevent unnecessary image reloading. This is always true for sprite/stage assets since they're loaded automatically.
      Parameters:
      path - the file path
      Returns:
      true if an image at the given path is already loaded by this manager, false otherwise

    • getLoadedImage

      Image getLoadedImage(Path path)
      Returns an image previously loaded from the given path, if any, otherwise returns null.
      Parameters:
      path - the file path
      Returns:
      the respective image or null
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if graphics manager is closed

    • destroyImage

      void destroyImage(Path path)
      If an image is loaded at the given path, closes and removes it, otherwise does nothing.
      Parameters:
      path - the file path
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if graphics manager is closed

    • runLater

      void runLater(Runnable run)
      Schedules the given task to run in the graphics loop.

      This is required for calling most RenderingSurface methods, since they can only change properties & render within a single thread.

      Parameters:
      run - the action
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if run == null
      IllegalStateException - if the work queue cannot accept more tasks (optional)

    • tick

      void tick()
      Advances the graphics manager by one tick. This method is called regularly from the graphics loop, no need to call in manually.
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if graphics manager is closed

    • close

      void close()
      This method is not expected to throw any checked exceptions. After calling this method, the graphics manager must not be used. Calling this method more than once does not have any effect.
      Specified by:
      close in interface AutoCloseable
      Specified by:
      close in interface NonThrowingAutoCloseable
